title: Netflix Executive Explains Cancelling Duffer Bros. New Sci-Fi Series With 1.2 Billion Minutes
summary: Netflix executive Jinny Howe addresses the cancellation of The Boroughs, despite the Duffer Bros. show clocking 1.2 billion minutes of viewership.

fullArticle:
Ryan Northrup is a Senior Staff Writer on ScreenRant's Movie & TV News team, where he has authored nearly 5,000 articles, including features and reviews. A graduate of McMaster University, Ryan began his career in public relations before attending Vancouver Film School. There, he built lasting industry connections and optioned a feature film screenplay.

The Boroughs' surprise cancellation gets a new explanation from a Netflix executive.

Created by Jeffrey Addiss and Will Matthews, the anticipated sci-fi show features the Duffer brothers as executive producers. The Duffers, of course, are best known for shepherding Netflix's Stranger Things to become a massive five-season success, but The Boroughs was cancelled last month after only one season.
